mappings.rationale: Directly covers materials processing, property modification, and the science of industrial materials central to this section.
Covers the engineering principles behind production processes including forming, joining, and material removal.
Relevant to metallurgy, welding processes, and the structural testing of metals and materials.
Covers the chemical processing methods (leaching, smelting, polymerization) used in preliminary processing of raw materials.
Relevant to heat treatment processes, pyrometallurgy, and the thermodynamic principles underlying materials processing.
Relevant to extractive metallurgy and the separation of metallic elements from mineral form.
Relevant to chemical processing including polymerization and the adhesive bonding of materials.
Relevant to metallography and the study of crystalline structures in metals during physical metallurgy.
Relevant to automated materials handling systems and the mechanization of production processes.
Relevant to advanced surface finishing processes and property modification techniques at the nanoscale.
Covers the historical development of metallurgy, iron and steel production, and industrial processing techniques.
Relevant to mineral processing including crushing and grinding of ores and concentration of metallic minerals.
